status_definitions.blocked: Cannot continue due to a real dependency or missing decision.
status_definitions.done: Acceptance checks passed and evidence exists in progress.md.

tasks.title: Bootstrap minimum harness governance files
acceptance_checks: All governance files exist in the workspace root.
The task board is machine-readable and dependency-aware.
A new session can determine the next task from init.ps1.
tasks.notes: Initial harness setup completed before implementation.

tasks.title: Align governance files with PRD v0.3
acceptance_checks: Governance files reference the manual-trigger P0 chain.
P0 no longer assumes automatic detection or Jira integration.
The next implementation task is based on the v0.3 PRD.
tasks.notes: Completed after the PRD was rewritten as a pure product document.

tasks.title: Draft P0 technical specification and schemas
acceptance_checks: Tech spec defines module layout, environment variables, callback route, and request flow.
Schema doc defines structured summary, citations, error replies, and analysis contracts.
P0 remains scoped to manual trigger, one-shot analysis, and thread reply.
tasks.notes: Completed to give future coding sessions enough structure to start writing code.

tasks.title: Draft P0 feature list and test cases
acceptance_checks: Feature list defines the detailed P0 implementation surface.
Test cases cover happy path, insufficient-context, and temporary-failure behavior.
The startup flow requires new sessions to read both documents.
tasks.notes: Completed to make future implementation and evaluation less ambiguous.

tasks.title: Draft P0 API contracts and prompt definitions
acceptance_checks: API contracts define Feishu and LLM integration boundaries for P0.
Jira remains explicitly out of scope for active P0 implementation.
Prompt definitions lock the structured summary and degraded response behavior.
tasks.notes: Completed to reduce guessing around external integrations and prompt behavior.

tasks.title: Add evolution governance, session playbook, and decision log
acceptance_checks: The project has explicit rules for controlled self-improvement.
The project defines when to start a new thread and how to bootstrap it.
Confirmed high-value project decisions are recorded in a reusable log.
tasks.notes: Completed to keep long-running vibecoding sessions recoverable and disciplined.

tasks.title: Create Python project skeleton and P0 module layout
acceptance_checks: The workspace has a runnable FastAPI application skeleton.
The module layout matches tech-spec.md.
A new developer can locate callback, thread, knowledge, analysis, and reply modules quickly.
tasks.notes: Completed with a uv-managed Python scaffold, FastAPI entrypoint, health route, base settings, and placeholder module boundaries.

tasks.title: Implement Feishu callback endpoint and manual command parsing
acceptance_checks: The app can receive and acknowledge Feishu callback payloads.
Supported manual commands are recognized from group messages or threads.
Unsupported or irrelevant messages are ignored safely.
tasks.notes: Completed with callback verification handling, normalized message event parsing, safe ignore behavior, and fixture-backed command parsing tests.

tasks.title: Implement current-thread context loading and normalization
acceptance_checks: The system can build one normalized analysis request from a triggered thread.
The normalized thread model matches schema.md.
Empty or malformed thread content is handled without crashing.
tasks.notes: Completed with a thread reader service, adapter-facing thread load contract, fallback-to-trigger behavior, and fixture-backed normalization tests.

tasks.title: Implement local knowledge loading and citation lookup
acceptance_checks: Local Markdown or text knowledge documents can be loaded from the configured directory.
The retrieval path can return citation candidates relevant to a triggered thread.
Citation objects match schema.md.
tasks.notes: Completed with recursive local document loading, graceful file-skip behavior, simple keyword retrieval, and citation object construction.

tasks.title: Implement one-shot structured summary generation
acceptance_checks: One triggered thread can produce a structured summary matching schema.md.
The result includes citations when evidence exists.
Insufficient-context cases do not fabricate strong conclusions.
tasks.notes: Completed with an OpenAI-compatible LLM adapter, prompt-driven summary generation, schema validation, insufficient-context fallback, and temporary-failure handling.

tasks.title: Implement Feishu thread reply rendering
acceptance_checks: The structured summary can be rendered into a readable Feishu reply.
The reply is sent back to the original thread or message context.
Failure replies follow the error response format defined in schema.md.
tasks.notes: Completed with success and failure render templates, a Feishu reply send adapter, and transport-backed tests for send success and failure behavior.

tasks.title: Add P0 acceptance fixtures and validation flow
acceptance_checks: A reviewer can run the P0 happy path against fixtures.
At least one insufficient-context case is validated.
P0 readiness can be evaluated without guessing.
tasks.notes: Completed with smoke fixtures for happy and insufficient-context flows, a dedicated smoke script, and a concise manual validation checklist.

tasks.title: Add same-thread follow-up analysis support
acceptance_checks: Users can ask for a rerun or updated summary in the same thread.
The product responds in a way that preserves thread continuity.
The follow-up behavior remains aligned with the PRD.
tasks.notes: Completed with follow-up context in the analysis request contract, previous-summary extraction from the same thread, follow-up-aware prompt construction, and updated reply prefixes.

tasks.title: Add conclusion summary and todo draft output
acceptance_checks: The product can generate a clearer closing summary for a settled thread.
Todo drafts are explicit but not automatically synced.
The output stays structured and source-aware.
tasks.notes: Completed with optional closing-summary and todo-draft fields in the structured summary contract, summarize-thread-specific prompt requirements, rendered draft output, and regression coverage.

tasks.title: Add external task sync and richer knowledge sources
acceptance_checks: Users can choose to sync external tasks from a prepared draft.
Additional knowledge sources improve available evidence without breaking P0 behavior.
The product remains manually controlled for high-risk actions.
tasks.notes: Completed with a confirmation-gated external task sync contract and service, a generic task sync client boundary, structured .knowledge.json bundle support for richer citations, and fixture-backed validation.

tasks.title: Add postmortem draft generation
acceptance_checks: A concluded discussion can generate a reusable postmortem draft.
The draft remains grounded in cited discussion and knowledge sources.
The feature does not weaken the simpler P0/P1 product flow.
tasks.notes: Completed with a dedicated postmortem draft contract, an LLM-backed service with summary-grounded fallback generation, a reviewable renderer, and fixture-backed tests. Post-plan follow-up later wired the live Feishu callback route into the real fetch/analyze/reply chain.

tasks.title: Realign product docs to evolving workflow agent roadmap
acceptance_checks: Core docs no longer describe the product as incident-summary-only.
The roadmap explicitly includes controlled growth and AI code review.
The repository still preserves approval and audit boundaries.
tasks.notes: Completed by repositioning the repository around a controlled workflow agent with incident and AI code review workflows on top of a shared growth kernel.

tasks.title: Add explicit memory foundation for workflow continuity
acceptance_checks: Follow-up analysis can load explicit thread state instead of relying only on marker heuristics.
Memory persistence is tenant-safe and thread-safe.
Current incident flow remains backward-compatible when memory is absent.
tasks.notes: Completed with a file-backed memory service under app/services/kernel, explicit thread-memory models, live-flow persistence after successful replies, backward-compatible heuristic fallback, and regression coverage.

tasks.title: Upgrade retrieval into planner-router-ranker pipeline
acceptance_checks: Release-related incident threads prefer release-note evidence over generic docs.
Weak evidence no longer bypasses insufficient-context safeguards.
Retrieval remains deterministic and locally testable.
tasks.notes: Completed with a deterministic retrieval package under app/services/retrieval, a stable knowledge-base facade, weak-evidence filtering, bounded second-pass recall, and regression coverage for route preference and insufficient-context safety.

tasks.title: Add action proposal queue and approval-backed incident actions
acceptance_checks: Task and postmortem proposals are reviewable before execution.
High-risk actions require explicit user confirmation.
Approved actions can be executed and written back to the thread.
tasks.notes: Completed with a file-backed thread-scoped action queue under app/services/kernel, summarize-thread action proposal creation, explicit approval commands like '批准动作 A1', and approval-backed task-sync/postmortem write-back coverage.

tasks.title: Add controlled growth kernel with recorder and skill candidates
acceptance_checks: Workflow runs produce auditable evidence records.
Reusable patterns can be stored as draft skill candidates.
No candidate skill becomes active without approval.
tasks.notes: Completed with append-only thread evidence, tenant audit logs, a draft-only skill registry under data/skills, deterministic pattern mining for repeated approval loops, and lifecycle guards that prevent activation before approval.

tasks.title: Launch AI code review MVP on the shared growth kernel
acceptance_checks: Users can manually submit a diff or patch for review.
The system returns structured, evidence-backed findings.
Review publication remains approval-gated.
tasks.notes: Completed with a Feishu-first AI review flow, manual PR or patch trigger parsing, deterministic diff normalization, structured draft findings, optional GitHub PR diff fetch, approval-backed GitHub draft comment publishing, and workflow-router wiring that keeps incident and review flows separate.

tasks.title: Grow review reuse safely with policy routing and adoption signals
acceptance_checks: Teams can bias review focus toward bug risk, test gaps, or security-oriented checks.
The system records whether review findings were published, accepted, or ignored.
Review-specific reusable patterns still remain candidate-first and approval-gated.
tasks.notes: Completed with explicit review-focus routing, repeated-request preference memory, review-state persistence, explicit finding feedback commands, and review-focus draft skill candidates mined from accepted findings.

tasks.title: Expand org memory and team-style shaping across incident and review workflows
acceptance_checks: Org-level conventions can shape review focus defaults without overriding explicit user requests.
Approved team conventions can shape postmortem and review output structure.
The new memory layer remains tenant-scoped, auditable, and does not silently rewrite canonical docs.
tasks.notes: Completed with an OrgConventionService, org-level review default focus shaping, team-style postmortem prompt and renderer shaping, tenant-scoped org-memory helpers, and regression coverage that keeps explicit user commands above stored defaults.

tasks.title: Add canonical knowledge gateway for approved org conventions and review policy
deliverables: Knowledge-base integration for canonical org policy
acceptance_checks: Org review defaults and postmortem style can be sourced from approved canonical docs instead of only mutable org memory payloads.
Canonical conventions take precedence over ad-hoc org memory while explicit user commands still win at runtime.
The gateway remains tenant-scoped, auditable, and does not let chat transcripts silently rewrite canonical policy.
tasks.notes: Completed with a tenant-scoped canonical convention service under the knowledge layer, runtime precedence of canonical docs over mutable org memory, shared KnowledgeBase integration for canonical policy docs, and postmortem style snapshots that keep approval-time write-back stable.

tasks.title: Add approval-backed convention promotion into the canonical gateway
acceptance_checks: Approved conventions can be promoted into tenant-scoped canonical docs only through explicit approval.
Canonical promotion writes versioned artifacts and audit entries instead of silently mutating runtime memory.
New runtime requests can use promoted canonical docs while previously created pending actions remain stable through snapshots.
tasks.notes: Completed with a promotion request contract, a new convention promotion service, explicit Feishu promotion commands that create pending approval actions, versioned canonical doc writes, candidate activation on successful promotion, and regression coverage for proposal, approval, and version retention.

tasks.title: Add GitHub-side review outcome ingestion and richer adoption signals
acceptance_checks: Published review comments can be reconciled with GitHub-side outcome signals instead of relying only on in-thread explicit feedback.
The growth kernel can distinguish published, accepted, ignored, and unresolved review outcomes with explicit source attribution.
Repo-side outcome ingestion remains approval-safe, tenant-scoped, and does not infer acceptance from silence.
tasks.notes: This next milestone should reduce reliance on manual Feishu feedback by pulling higher-quality review outcome signals from the repo side.
